items.question: What is the primary function of the 'Transformer' model architecture described in the figure?
options: To map word vectors directly to output probabilities without intermediate processing
To rely entirely on recurrent and convolutional neural networks for sequence modeling
To handle input and output sequences using attention and feed-forward networks without recurrence or convolution
To compress sequences into a single fixed-size vector before processing
items.explanation: The title 'Transformer' and the context of the excerpt (referencing the well-known architecture associated with Figure 1 in the literature) define the model by its use of attention and lack of recurrence or convolutions.

items.question: Based on the label 'Figure 1: The Transformer - model architecture', what structural implication is standard for this diagram?
options: It depicts a linear stack of layers where input flows only left-to-right
It consists of an Encoder stack on the left and a Decoder stack on the right
It shows a flat structure with no distinct sub-components
It represents a purely unsupervised clustering mechanism
items.explanation: The referenced Figure 1 from the seminal paper describes the overall architecture, which is structurally composed of an Encoder and a Decoder.

items.question: Which component is NOT a structural part of the 'Transformer' architecture shown in Figure 1?
items.explanation: The defining characteristic of the Transformer architecture depicted in Figure 1 is the complete removal of recurrent layers (like LSTMs or GRUs) in favor of attention mechanisms.
